title: How to Talk So Kids Will Listen & Listen So Kids Will Talk
author: Adele Faber & Elaine Mazlish
science_caveat: Communication-only; no neuroscience claims to be wrong
top_praise: Concrete scripts; the foundational text most later books copy
top_criticism: Dated (1980); reads as anti-yelling/anti-hitting boomer correction; obvious if you already parent gently
verdict: Foundational communication primer; the source others derive from

title: Good Inside
author: Becky Kennedy
science_caveat: Premise 'everyone is good inside' is a values claim not a finding; ~6 studies cited
top_praise: Compassionate; reduces parent guilt; 'connection + boundary' framing
top_criticism: Premise-dependent (worldview clash); no consequences; thin on what to do when it fails; repetitive
verdict: Best modern emotional-coaching guide IF you accept its premise; strong companion, weak standalone method

title: How to Talk So Little Kids Will Listen
author: Joanna Faber & Julie King
top_praise: Practical, age-2-7 specific tools that work immediately
top_criticism: Extremely repetitive ('could be a blog post'); permissive feel; manipulative-distraction criticism
verdict: Most practical for toddlers; skim the examples

title: No-Drama Discipline
author: Daniel J. Siegel & Tina Payne Bryson
science_caveat: Brain-based framing; critics cite permissive-parenting research showing worse outcomes
top_praise: 'Discipline = teach not punish' reframe is genuinely useful
top_criticism: Seen as 'No Discipline'/permissive; rehash of Whole-Brain Child; padded; anecdotal
verdict: Useful reframe buried in repetition; redundant if you read Whole-Brain Child

title: The Whole-Brain Child
author: Daniel J. Siegel & Tina Payne Bryson
evidence_grounding: Weak-Moderate (Siegel is a researcher; book is pop-neuro)
science_caveat: Leans on DEBUNKED left/right-brain split + 'lizard brain' pop-neuroscience; missing citations; a factual error (fever=98.6)
top_praise: Accessible brain framing; 'name it to tame it'; charts
top_criticism: Out-of-date/oversimplified science; uncited claims; repetitive; copies How to Talk
verdict: Engaging entry to brain-based parenting but read its science skeptically

title: Bringing Up Bébé
author: Pamela Druckerman
science_caveat: No data; cultural generalization from one expat's Paris circle
top_praise: Witty; 'The Pause'; permission to keep adult/marriage priorities
top_criticism: Anecdotal; classist/fatphobic/culturally-essentialist callouts; smug; not a method
verdict: A memoir, not a parenting method; read for one or two ideas, not as science
